prelimsPoints: Bharat Shakti is a tri-services exercise (Army, Navy, Air Force).
Held at Pokhran Field Firing Ranges, Rajasthan.
Focuses on integrated combat capabilities and indigenous weapon systems.
Aims to enhance interoperability and strategic readiness.
Fourth edition of the exercise.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of joint military exercises like 'Bharat Shakti' in enhancing India's defence preparedness and strategic capabilities.
Analyze the role of indigenization in defence manufacturing (Atmanirbhar Bharat) as showcased in such exercises.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in achieving seamless interoperability among India's three armed services.
Evaluate India's position as a regional security provider in the context of its evolving military doctrines and capabilities.
How do such exercises contribute to deterrence and regional stability?
GS3.imageDescription: Indian military personnel from Army, Navy, and Air Force conducting a joint exercise with tanks, aircraft, and warships.
GS3.trivia: Pokhran is famously known for being the site of India's nuclear weapons tests in 1974 and 1998.

GS3.headline: RBI Releases Annual Report on Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) Implementation
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) today released its annual report detailing the implementation and enforcement of the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA), 1999, for the fiscal year 2024-25.
• The report highlighted trends in foreign exchange transactions, capital flows, and compliance levels among entities operating under FEMA regulations.
• It noted a significant increase in foreign direct investment (FDI) inflows and a stable outward remittances landscape, attributing it to robust regulatory oversight and economic confidence.
• The RBI also outlined key enforcement actions taken against non-compliant entities and proposed minor amendments to further streamline FEMA's provisions.
• This annual review is crucial for policymakers to assess the effectiveness of India's foreign exchange regime and its alignment with global best practices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Investment models
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: RBI releases annual report on FEMA implementation.
FEMA, 1999, regulates foreign exchange transactions.
Report covers trends in FDI, capital flows, and compliance.
RBI proposes minor amendments to FEMA.
FEMA replaced FERA (Foreign Exchange Regulation Act).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of FEMA in regulating India's foreign exchange market and facilitating international trade and investment.
Analyze the trends in FDI and capital flows as highlighted by the RBI report and their implications for the Indian economy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's regulatory and enforcement mechanisms under FEMA.
Examine the impact of global economic trends and geopolitical events on India's foreign exchange management.
Suggest reforms or policy changes to further liberalize or strengthen India's foreign exchange regime.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo with graphs depicting economic trends in the background.
GS3.trivia: FEMA came into effect on June 1, 2000, replacing the more stringent FERA, 1973, to liberalize India's foreign exchange regime.

GS3.headline: New Guidelines for Green Hydrogen Production Incentives Released by MNRE
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) issued detailed guidelines for production incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The incentives aim to reduce the cost of green hydrogen production, making it competitive with fossil fuel-based alternatives and accelerating its adoption across various sectors.
• The guidelines specify eligibility criteria for projects, performance benchmarks, and the mechanism for disbursing financial support to manufacturers of electrolyzers and producers of green hydrogen.
• This move is crucial for positioning India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to its decarbonization goals.
• Industry stakeholders have welcomed the clarity, expecting a boost in investments and technological advancements in the green hydrogen ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)

mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change mitigation efforts.

GS3.imageDescription: A futuristic image of a green hydrogen production plant with renewable energy sources like solar panels and wind turbines.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

GS3.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Venus Orbiter Mission (Shukrayaan-1) Launch in Mid-2025
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced that preparations for its ambitious Venus Orbiter Mission, 'Shukrayaan-1', are in advanced stages for a mid-2025 launch.
• Shukrayaan-1 aims to study the atmosphere and surface of Venus, focusing on its dense sulfuric acid clouds, volcanic activity, and atmospheric composition.
• The mission will carry several indigenous scientific instruments, including a high-resolution radar and a spectrometer, to gather unprecedented data from Venus.
• This mission marks India's first foray into exploring Venus, building on the success of its Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan).
• The scientific data collected by Shukrayaan-1 is expected to provide crucial insights into planetary evolution and the habitability of extreme environments.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology

Analyze the role of ISRO in advancing India's technological capabilities and global standing in space research.

What are the future prospects for India's space exploration programs beyond Mars and Venus?
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 spacecraft orbiting Venus, with its scientific instruments deployed.
GS3.trivia: Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to its similar size and mass, but it has an extremely hot, toxic atmosphere.

GS3.headline: Government Reviews Initiatives for Promoting Millets in Public Distribution System (PDS)
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution conducted a high-level review of initiatives aimed at integrating millets into the Public Distribution System (PDS) across various states.
• The review assessed the progress of procurement, storage, and distribution of millets, particularly jowar, bajra, and ragi, to enhance nutritional security.
• Challenges identified included supply chain management, consumer acceptance in some regions, and the need for standardized processing facilities.
• The government plans to expand the pilot projects and increase awareness campaigns about the nutritional benefits of millets, especially in regions with high malnutrition.
• This push aligns with the International Year of Millets (IYM) 2023 objectives and India's commitment to food security and diversified agriculture.
gsSubTopics: Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security

Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

GS3.imageDescription: Various types of millets displayed in bowls, with PDS ration cards in the background.
GS3.trivia: India is the largest producer of millets in the world, accounting for over 40% of global production.

GS3.headline: India Proposes Global AI Governance Summit, Emphasizes Ethical AI Development
GS3.explainer:
• India formally proposed hosting a Global AI Governance Summit in late 2025, aiming to bring together nations to discuss a unified framework for ethical AI development and regulation.
• The proposal emphasizes the need for international cooperation to address challenges posed by rapidly advancing Artificial Intelligence, including bias, privacy concerns, and autonomous weapons.
• India highlighted its 'AI for All' approach, advocating for inclusive AI development that benefits humanity while mitigating risks.
• The summit would seek to build consensus on common principles, standards, and best practices for responsible AI innovation and deployment.
• This initiative positions India as a key player in shaping the global discourse on AI governance and ensuring a human-centric approach to technology.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Cyber security

GS3.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956, defining it as 'the science and engineering of making intelligent machines'.
